Thanks for the opening proposal. That level is well above where we can make the economics work on our side, even allowing for current market firmness, so I need to start much lower at 2.12 per unit. If you can move materially toward a workable range, I can keep the discussion active and look at whether volume planning or a steadier call-off schedule helps narrow the gap. The supplier opened far above the buyer's workable economics, so an aggressive counter is the reasonable next move rather than accepting or walking away. A low anchor preserves room to negotiate while signaling that price must come down substantially before a deal is possible.

I appreciate the counter, but 2.12 is far too low relative to current market conditions and our supply economics. The latest market reference we are seeing is materially higher, so I can move down from 6.45 to 5.95 per unit as a good-faith concession. If you can come up meaningfully from your opening level, I’m willing to keep working on structure and volume commitments to see whether we can close the gap. The buyer’s offer is above our internal floor but still far below both our last ask and the latest market reference, so accepting would leave too much value on the table this early. A moderate concession keeps the negotiation active, signals flexibility, and uses the strong market backdrop to justify holding a substantially higher price.

Thanks for the movement from 6.45 to 5.95, but that level is still far outside a workable range for us on a manufacturing buy. We are seeing too much distance between your ask and the economics we need to preserve downstream, so I can improve modestly to 2.22 per unit as a serious step from our opening position. If you have flexibility tied to volume or term commitments, bring your number down substantially and we can keep pushing toward a structure that works for both sides. The supplier made a concession but remains extremely far from the buyer's feasible range. A small increase keeps the negotiation alive, signals some flexibility, and avoids ending talks prematurely despite a strong market reference favoring the seller.

Thanks for the movement, but 5.35 is still far outside a range we can support on our side.
